children.name: Iguana
children.description: True iguanas.

children.name: Amblyrhynchus
children.description: The marine iguana.

children.name: Brachylophus
children.description: Fiji iguanas.

children.name: Brachylophus bulabula
children.description: Brachylophus bulabula, commonly known as the Central Fijian banded iguana is a species of iguanid lizard endemic to some of the larger central and northwestern islands of Fiji (Ovalau, Kadavu and Viti Levu), where it occurs in Fijian wet forest.

children.name: Brachylophus fasciatus
children.description: Brachylophus fasciatus, the Lau banded iguana, is an arboreal species of lizard endemic to the Lau Islands of the eastern part of the Fijian archipelago.

children.name: Brachylophus vitiensis
children.description: The Fiji crested iguana or Fijian crested iguana is a critically endangered species of iguana native to some of the northwestern islands of the Fijiian archipelago, where it is found in dry forest on Yadua Taba, Yadua, Macuata, Yaquaga, Devuilau, Malolo, Monu and Monuriki.

children.name: Cachryx
children.description: Yucatán spiny-tailed iguanas.

children.name: Cachryx alfredschmidti
children.description: The Campeche spiny-tailed iguana is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae. The species is native to southeastern Mexico and adjacent Guatemala.

children.name: Cachryx defensor
children.description: The Yucatán spiny-tailed iguana is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae. It is endemic to northern Yucatán, Mexico.

children.name: Conolophus
children.description: Galápagos land iguanas.

children.name: Conolophus subcristatus
children.description: The Galápagos land iguana is a very large species of lizard in the family Iguanidae, and one of three species of the genus Conolophus. It is endemic to the Galápagos Islands off of Ecuador's Pacific coast, inhabiting the dry lowlands of Fernandina, Isabela, Santa Cruz, North Seymour, Baltra, and South Plaza islands.

children.name: Conolophus marthae
children.description: Conolophus marthae, also known commonly as the Galápagos pink land iguana, is a species of lizard of the family Iguanidae. This critically endangered iguana is native only to the Wolf Volcano in northern Isabela Island of the Galápagos Islands (Ecuador). It has a pink body with some dark stripes, prompting some to call it the pink iguana or the Galápagos rosy iguana.

children.name: Conolophus pallidus
children.description: Conolophus pallidus, also known commonly as the Santa Fe land iguana and the Barrington land iguana, is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae. It is one of three species in the genus Conolophus and is endemic to Santa Fe Island in the Galapagos.

children.name: Ctenosaura
children.description: Spiny-tailed iguanas.

children.name: Ctenosaura similis
children.description: Ctenosaura similis, commonly known as the black iguana or black spiny-tailed iguana, is an iguanid lizard native to Mexico and Central America. It has been reported in some Colombian islands in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean, and has been introduced to the United States in the state of Florida. The largest species in the genus Ctenosaura, it is commonly found in areas such as grasslands and forests.

children.name: Ctenosaura acanthura
children.description: Ctenosaura acanthura, is a species of iguanid lizard found in eastern Mexico and extreme western Guatemala. The standardized English name is the Mexican spiny-tailed iguana. Confusingly however, an earlier edition of standardized names for Mexican herpetofauna called Ctenosaura acanthura the northeastern spinytailed iguana and applied the name Mexican spinytailed iguana to Ctenosaura pectinata, which was called the western spiny-tailed iguana in the second edition.

children.name: Ctenosaura bakeri
children.description: Ctenosaura bakeri, also known as the Utila spiny-tailed iguana, Baker's spinytail iguana, swamper or wishiwilly del suampo, is a critically endangered species of spinytail iguana endemic to the island of Utila, one of the Islas de la Bahía off the coast of Honduras in the Caribbean.

children.name: Ctenosaura brachylopha
children.description: Yellowish body coloration distinguishes it from the closely related C. pectinata; ranges from Sinaloa to Nayarit on the Pacific coast of Mexico, including Isla Clarión; resurrected from synonymy in 2019 based on mitochondrial and microsatellite genetic data.

children.name: Ctenosaura clarki
children.description: Ctenosaura clarki, commonly known as the Balsas armed lizard, Balsas spiny-tailed iguana, Michoacán dwarf spiny-tailed iguana, or nopiche, is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae. The species native to Mexico.

children.name: Ctenosaura conspicuosa
children.description: Ctenosaura conspicuosa, commonly known as the San Esteban spinytail iguana is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae.

children.name: Ctenosaura flavidorsalis
children.description: Ctenosaura flavidorsalis, commonly known as the yellow-backed spiny-tailed iguana, is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae.

children.name: Ctenosaura hemilopha
children.description: Ctenosaura hemilopha, also known as the Cape spiny-tailed iguana or Baja California spiny-tailed iguana, is a species of spinytail iguana endemic to Baja California.

children.name: Ctenosaura macrolopha
children.description: Ctenosaura macrolopha, the Sonoran spiny-tailed iguana, is a species of iguana native to Mexico.

children.name: Ctenosaura melanosterna
children.description: Ctenosaura melanosterna, commonly known as the black-chested spiny-tailed iguana or , is a species of iguana in the genus Ctenosaura.

children.name: Ctenosaura nolascensis
children.description: Ctenosaura nolascensis, the Nolasco spiny-tailed iguana or San Pedro Nolasco spinytail iguana, is a species of iguana native to Mexico.

children.name: Ctenosaura oaxacana
children.description: Ctenosaura oaxacana, commonly known as the Oaxacan spiny-tailed iguana, is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae.

children.name: Ctenosaura oedirhina
children.description: Ctenosaura oedirhina, commonly known as the Roatán spiny-tailed iguana or de Queiroz's spiny-tailed iguana, is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae.

children.name: Ctenosaura palearis
children.description: Ctenosaura palearis, commonly known as the Motagua spiny-tailed iguana, is a species of spiny-tailed iguana endemic to the Motagua Valley in Guatemala.

children.name: Ctenosaura pectinata
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = Ctenosaura pectinata (24333259842).jpg | image_caption = Ctenosaura pectinata from the coast southwest Mexico | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Ctenosaura | species = pectinata | authority = (Wiegmann, 1834) | synonyms = * Cyclura pectinata Wiegmann, 1834 * Ctenosaura pectinata | synonyms_ref = }}.

children.name: Ctenosaura quinquecarinata
children.description: Ctenosaura quinquecarinata, commonly known as the Oaxacan spinytail iguana or the five-keeled spiny-tailed iguana is a species of lizard in the family Iguanidae native to Central America.

children.name: Cyclura
children.description: Caribbean rock iguanas.

children.name: Cyclura cychlura
children.description: cychlura Category:Endemic fauna of the Bahamas Category:Reptiles of the Bahamas Category:Endangered fauna of North America.

children.name: Cyclura nubila
children.description: The Cuban rock iguana (Cyclura nubila), also known as the Cuban ground iguana or Cuban iguana, is a species of lizard of the iguana family.

children.name: Cyclura onchiopsis
children.description: Now extinct; formerly endemic to Navassa Island in the Caribbean, between Hispaniola and Jamaica; had prominent temporal swellings and likely shared the rhinoceros-like horn of its closest relatives; last recorded in the 1960s; habitat destruction from guano mining and introduced predators caused its demise.

children.name: Cyclura pinguis
children.description: Cyclura pinguis, the Anegada rock iguana, Anegada ground iguana or stout iguana, is a critically endangered species of lizard of the genus Cyclura belonging to the family Iguanidae. The species can be found exclusively in the islands of Anegada and Guana. Historically, it inhabited the islands of Puerto Rico and Saint Thomas; however, the animal's original range has been greatly diminished over prehistory.

children.name: Cyclura ricordii
children.description: Cyclura ricordii, also known as Ricord's ground iguana or Ricord's rock iguana, is an endangered species of medium-sized rock iguana, a large herbivorous lizard.

children.name: Cyclura rileyi
children.description: {{Speciesbox | image = Cyclura rileyi rileyi Green Cay c W K Hayes 2004.JPG | status = EN| status_system = IUCN2.3 | status_ref = | status2 = CITES_A1 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Cyclura | species = rileyi | authority = Stejneger, 1903 | subdivision_ranks = Subspecies | subdivision = * C.

children.name: Dipsosaurus
children.description: Heat-tolerant desert iguanas.

children.name: Sauromalus
children.description: Chuckwallas.

children.name: Sauromalus ater
children.description: {{Speciesbox | name = Common chuckwalla | image = Sauromalus ater Phoenix.jpg | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = G5 | status2_system = TNC | status2_ref = | taxon = Sauromalus ater | authority = Dumeril, 1856 | range_map = Sauromalus ater distribution.svg | range_map_caption = Approximate range of S.

children.name: Sauromalus klauberi
children.description: Sauromalus klauberi, commonly called the Catalina chuckwalla or the spotted chuckwalla, is a species of chuckwalla, a lizard in the family Iguanidae.

children.name: Sauromalus slevini
children.description: Sauromalus slevini, also known as the Monserrat chuckwalla or Slevin's chuckwalla, is a species of chuckwalla belonging to the family Iguanidae.
